1437  (XIV).  Coordination,  sur le  plan  adminis› tratif  et  budgetai~  de  l'action  de  l'Orga· nisation  des  Natioll8  Unies et de  celle  des institutions  specialisees
text_en: 1437 (XIV). Coordination, on the administrative and budgetary level, of the activities of the United Nations and of the specialized institutions

text_fr: L'Assemblee generale,

E u  egard aux rapports que le  Comite consultatif pour les  questions  administratives  et  budgetaires  a  etablis au cours de  ces  dernieres  annees  sur  la question  de  la coordination administrative et budgetaire entre les orga› nisations  et  Jes  programmes  des  Nations  Unies,  et considerant  qu'il est  souhaitable que  le  Comite  consul› tatif continue a s'occuper de  cette  question,
text_en: Having regard to the reports of the Advisory Committee on Administrative and Budgetary Questions during the past few years on the question of administrative and budgetary coordination between the United Nations agencies and its programmes, and considering that it is desirable that the Comité consulsatif continue to deal with this issue,

text_fr: I

items_fr: 1. Exprime  sa  satisfaction au  Comite  consultatif pour  les  questions  administratives  et  budgetaires  des rapports  qu'il  a etablis, en  particulier  du  rapport relatif  aux  budgets  d'administration  des  institutions specialisees  pour  1960 25 et  du  rapport  d'ensemble sur les  etudes  speciales effectuees  par le  Comite consultatif au  siege  de  ces  institutions 28 ;

2. Exprime  egalemuit  sa  satisfaction aux  institu› tions specialisees et a l'Agence internationale de l'energie atomique  pour  la  cooperation  et  les  facilites  qu'elles ont  offertes  au  Comite  consultatif a !'occasion  de  ses etudes  et  visites;

3. Appelle  l' attention de  l' Agence  internationale  de l'energie  atomique  et  de  !'Union  internationale  des telecommunications  sur  Jes  rapports  du  Comite  con› sultatif  relatifs  aux etudes  speciales  effectuees  au  siege de ces institutions 27 ;
items_en: 1. Expresses its appreciation to the Advisory Committee on Administrative and Budgetary Questions for the reports it has prepared, in particular the report on the administrative budgets of the specialized institutions for 1960 25 and the comprehensive report on special studies carried out by the Advisory Committee at the session of those institutions; 28

2. Expresses its satisfaction with the specialized institutions and the International Atomic Energy Agency for Cooperation and the facilities they have provided to the Advisory Committee on the occasion of its studies and visits;

3. Draws the attention of the International Atomic Energy Agency and the International Telecommunications Union to the reports of the Committee on Special Studies carried out at the seat of these institutions; 27

text_fr: 1436  (XIV).  Repartition  geographique  du  per› &onnel  du Secretariat  de !'Organisation des Nations  Unies
text_en: 1436 (XIV). Geographical distribution of the staff of the Secretariat of the United Nations

text_fr: L' A.rsemblee  generale,
Rappelant les  dispositions  de  l' Article 101 de  la Charte des  Nations Unies,
text_en: Recalling the provisions of Article 101 of the Charter of the United Nations,

text_fr: Ayant  examine le rapport  du  Secretaire  general relatif a la  repartition  geographique  du  personnel  du Secretariat de !'Organisation des  Nations  U nies 24 ,
text_en: Having examined the report of the Secretary-General on the geographical distribution of the staff of the Secretariat of the United Nations 24 ,

items_fr: -NoJant  avec  satisfaction que,  pendant  l'annee 1959, un certain progres a  ete accompli en ce  qui concerne la mise  en  reuvre  de  ses  resolutions  1097  (XI)  du  27 fevrier  1957,  1226  (XII)  du  14  decembre  1957  et 1294  (XIII)  du  5  decembre  1958,
items_en: -NoJant with satisfaction that, during the year 1959, some progress has been made in the implementation of its Resolutions 1097 (XI) of 27 February 1957, 1226 (XII) of 14 December 1957 and 1294 (XIII) of 5 December 1958,

text_fr: N otant en  outre que le  Secretaire general poursuivra ses  efforts  en vue de  hater la  realisation  d'une reparti› tion geographique equitable du personnel du Secretariat,
text_en: Noting further that the Secretary-General will continue his efforts to secure the achievement of an equitable geographical rotation of the Secretariat's staff,

text_fr: Reconnaissant que,  sans une representation regionale et culturelle  satisfaisante a !'echelon  de  direction  du Secretariat, les  fins  de la Charte ne peuvent etre servies comme ii  convient,
text_en: Recognizing that, without a satisfactory regional and cultural representation of the Secretariat, the purposes of the Charter cannot be served as appropriate,

items_fr: I. Recommande que:

a) Lors du recrutement du personnel du Secretariat de !'Organisation des Nations Unies, priorite soit donnee aux candidats qualifies des Etats Membres qui ne comp› tent pas de ressortissants parmi  le  personnel ou qui  en comptent un nombre proportionnellement trop faible;

b) Les  vacances  de  pastes  eleves  du  Secretariat soient pou~vues,  clans  toute la mesure possible,  par des candidats  qualifies  representant  les  regions  geographi› ques et  les  principales  cultures  qui  ne  sont  pas  repre› sentees clans  ces  postes  clefs,  ou  qui  ne  le  sont  pas suffisamment ;
C) Chaque fois  que  cela  est  possible,  ii  soit  procede a des  echanges  de  personnel  plus  nombreux  entre  le Siege  et  les  bureaux  exterieurs  de  !'Organisation;
d) Les  efforts  du  Secretaire  general  en  vue  d'aug› menter  le  nombre  des  fonctionnaires  du  Secretariat

4. Appelle  egalement l' attention de  toutes  les  ins› titutions  specialisees  et  de  l' Agence  internationale  de l'energie atomique sur le  rapport d'ensemble du Comite consultatif et sur le  rapport relatif aux budgets d'admi› nistration  de  ces  institutions pour  1960;

5. Prie le  Comite consultatif  de continuer a etudier !'expansion  et  !'evolution  des  bureaux,  operations  et conferences de  !'Organisation des  Nations Unies et  des institutions,  en  dehors  de  leurs  sieges  respectifs,  et  de rendre  compte a l'Assemblee  generale,  !ors  de  sa quinzi€me session,  des  possibilites  <le  renforcer la coor› dination  sur le  plan  administratif et hmlgetaire;
items_en: I. Recommends that:

(a) When recruiting staff of the United Nations Secretariat, priority shall be given either to qualified candidates from Member States who are not nationals of the United Nations or who have a proportionately small number of such candidates;

(b) The vacations of the students of the Secretariat shall be provided, as far as possible, by qualified candidates representing the geographical regions and the main cultures which are not represented or which are not sufficiently represented by these key posts;
(C) Whenever possible, more staff exchanges between the Siege and the external offices of the Organization shall take place;
(d) The efforts of the Secretary-General to increase the number of Secretariat staff

4. Also draws the attention of all specialized institutions and the International Atomic Energy Agency to the comprehensive report of the Advisory Committee and to the report on the administration budgets of these institutions for 1960;

5. Requests the Advisory Committee to continue to study the expansion and evolution of the offices, operations and conferences of the United Nations and the institutions, outside their respective seats, and to report to the General Assembly, at its fifty-first session, on the possibilities of strengthening the administrative and humanitarian coordination of the programme;

text_fr: JI

items_fr: 1. Autorise le  Comite  consultatif  pour  Jes  questions administratives et budgetaires  iL  effectuer  des  etudes et a soumettre des  rapports  concrrnant  la  coordination et Jes  aspects administratifs et budgetaires des programmes speciaux  de  !'Organisation  des  Nations  U nies, a la demande  d'un  organe  principal  ou  de  l'organisme responsable  du  programme special  considere ;

2. Autorise  egalement le  Comite consultatif,  confor› mement a son mandat tel qu'il est defini par !'article 158 du  reglement  interieur  de  l'Assemblee generale, a se reunir, selon qu'il le  jugera necessaire et a propos, <lans Jes  divers bureaux de !'Organisation des  Nations Unies OU  au  siege  des  institutions  specialisees  et  de  l' Agence internationale de l'energie atomique et,  sur la  demande de  ces  institutions, a Jes conseiller  sur  Jes  questions administratives  et  financieres.
items_en: 1. Authorizes the Advisory Committee on Administrative and Budgetary Questions to carry out studies and to submit reports on coordination and administrative and budgetary aspects of the special programmes of the United Nations, at the request of a principal organ or body responsible for the special programme;

2. Authorizes also the Advisory Committee, in accordance with its mandate as defined by Article 158 of the Internal Rules of Procedure of the General Assembly, to meet, as it deems necessary and appropriate, at the session of the specialized institutions and the International Atomic Energy Agency and, at the request of these institutions, to advise on administrative and financial matters.

1438  (XIV).  Groupe  de  verificateurs  exterieurs des  eomptes
L' Assemblee gcnerale.

Ayant  examine le  rapport  du  Secretaire  general 28 relatif au Groupe mixte des  verificateurs  exterieurs des comptes cree par la  resolution 347  (IV) de  I' Assemblee generale,  en date du 24 novemhre  1949,
text_en: Having considered the report of the Secretary General 28 on the Joint Auditing Group established by Resolution 347 (IV) of the General Assembly of 24 November 1949,

text_fr: Decide que  les  dispositions  figurant  en  annexe a la presente  resolution  remplaceront  celles  de  l'appendice B  de  sa resolution 347  (IV).
text_en: Decides that the provisions set out in the annex to this resolution shall replace those set out in appendix B to its resolution 347 (IV).

text_fr: 846hn<' seance  pleniere, 5  dfrcmbrc  1959.
ANNEXE

items_fr: 1. Les  membres  du  ComitP  des  commissairl's  aux  comptes de  !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  et  ks  verificateurs  exte› rieurs  designes  par  les  institutions  specialisec,  et  par  I' Agencc internationale  de  I' encrgie  atnmiquc  constituent  un  Groupe  de verificateurs  exterieurs  de,  comptes  charge  de  facilitcr  la  coor› dination  des  verifications  confi{c,  i,  ses  membres  et  d'echange,· des  informations  sur  lcs  methodes  et  les  conclusions.

2. Le  Groupe  peut  soum<·ttre  aux  chefs  des  secretariats  des organisations  participantes  toutcs  les  observations  ou  recom› mandations  qu'il desire  faire  au  sujet  ,le  la  rnmptabilite  et  des methodes  financieres  des  organisations  interessees.

3. Les  chefs  des  secretariats  des  organisations  participantes peuvcnt,  par  l'intermediaire  de l<-ur commissaire  ( ou  leurs commissaires)  aux  comptcs,  sr ,umettre  au  ( ;roupe  toute  ques› tion  relevant  de  sa  competence  ,ur  laquelle  ils  ,!esirent  ubtenir son  avis  ou  ses  recommandations.

4. Le  Groupe  elit  son  president  et  adopte  son  reglement interieur.  II  tient  des  reunions  chaque  fois  que  besoin  en  est, mais  normalement  au  moins 1111e fois  tons  les  deux  alls.
5. Les  frais  de  reunion  du  Groupe  sont  a  la  charge  des organisatioris  participantes.
items_en: 1. The members of the Committee on Committees on the Accounts of the United Nations and external auditors drawn up by the specialized institutions and by the International Agency for the Encrgie atnmiquc shall constitute a Group of external verifiers of accounts which shall facilitate the coordination of the verifications entrusted to it, its members and exchange, information on methods and conclusions.

2. The Group may submit to the heads of the secretariats of the participating organizations any observations or recommendations it intends to make on the subject, the rnmptability and the financial methods of the interested organizations.

3. The heads of the secretariats of the participating organizations may, through the intermediary of the Commissioner (or their commissioners) to the accounts, submit to the ( ;roupe all questions falling within his competence, upon which they , intended to obtain his opinion or recommendations.
4. The Group elects its president and adopts its internal rules. It holds meetings whenever necessary, but normally at least 1111 times the two alls.
5. The meeting expenses of the Group shall be borne by the participating organizations.

text_fr: 1439  (XIV).  Ecole  internationale des  Nations  Unies
L' Asscmblec generate,
Ayant  examine le rapport  du  Secre~aire  ge~eral relatif a !'Ecole  internationale  des  Nat10ns  Umes 29 et  le  rapport  du  Conseil  d'administration de !'Ecole 30 ,
text_en: Having examined the report of the General Secretary on the International School of Nat10n Umes 29 and the report of the Board of Directors of School 30,

text_fr: Rappclant sa  resolution  1297  (XIII)  du  5  decembre 1958,  clans  laquelle  elle  priait  le  Secretaire  general  de preter ses  bons offices  pour aider l'Ecole a trouver des locaux  permanents  convenables  et  approuvait  l'octroi d'une subvention pour l'annee scolaire  1958-1959,
text_en: Revoking its resolution 1297 (XIII) of 5 December 1958, which it requested the Secretary General to provide his good offices to help the School find suitable permanent premises and approved a grant for the 1958-1959 school year,

text_fr: l?econnaissant qu'il  est  de  plus  en  plus  urgent  que !'Ecole ait des  locaux  permanents,
text_en: knowing that it is more and more urgent that!' School have permanent premises,

text_fr: Estimant ,1u'il  est  necessaire  d'assurer la  solvabilite de !'Ecole,
text_en: Estimating that it is necessary to ensure the solvency of !' School,

text_fr: N otant la  suggestion du Secretaire general tendant a creer, pour aider !'Ecole internationale, un fonds perma11e11t  qui  serait  finance  au  moyen des  benefices  nets  du comptoir  de  souvenirs  de  l'Organisation des  Nations l Tnies,
text_en: Not forgetting the suggestion of the Secretary General to create, in order to help!' International School, a perma11e11t fund which would be financed by means of net benefits from the souvenir counter of the United Nations,

items_fr: I. F.rprime ii nouveau l' espoir que des mesures seront prises  pour  permettre  aux  enfants  d'un nombre  aussi grand  que  possible  de  memhres  des  delegations,  de fonctionnaires  de  !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  et d'autres  personnes  en  rapport  avec  !'Organisation de frequenter  !'Ecole internationale  des  Nations  Unies;
2. Im·ifr le Conseil  d'administration  de  !'Ecole a cn;er 1111  Fonds de  !'Ecole internationale;
3. J)fridc de fournir au Fonds, pendant une periode de cinq ans, !'assistance financiere continue que l'Assem› hlee  generale pourra  juger necessaire;

4. ])fridc de  vt'rser au  Fon<ls  en  1960  une  contri› bution  de 100.000  dollars  pour  combler  le  deficit d'exploitation  accumule a la  fin de  l'annee  scolaire 1959-1960  et  pour  tout  autre  objet  concernant  !'Ecole que  le  Conseil  d'administration jugera  approprie;

5. Prie le  Secretaire  general  de  continuer a preter ses  bons  offices  au  Conseil  d'administration de  !'Ecole pour !'aider a trouver des  locaux  perrnanents convena› bles  pour  !'Ecole a proximite  immediate  du  Siege  de !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies, a etablir  Jes  plans d'un hatiment  et a poursuivrt' ses  efforts  pour obtenir de  sources  privees  Jes  foncls  necessaires a la construc› tion  du  hatiment  et,  le  cas  echeant. a !'acquisition du terrain;
6. Prfr le  Secretaire general de  presenter a I' Assem› blce generate, !ors de  sa quinzieme session :
a) Un rapport du Conseil d'administration de !'Ecole stir  la  creation  et  Jes  operations du  Fonds;

b) Des  recommandations,  accompagnees  des  obser› vations  du  Comite consultatif  pour  les  questions admi› nistratives  et  budgetaires,  sur  Jes  contributions  futures au  Fonds et sur les  moyens  de financer  lesdites  contri› butions,  compte  term  notamment  de  la  suggestion figurant aux paragraplws 8 t>t  <) du rapport du Secretaire general;
c) Un  rapport  sur  Jes  resultats  obtenus  en  ce  qui concerne  la  recherche  de  locaux  permanents  pour !'Ecole.
items_en: I. F.rprime ii new the hope that measures will be taken to enable the children of as many delegations as possible to memhrs, United Nations officials and other persons in connection with the Organization to attend the United Nations International School;
2. Improve the Board of Directors of the School to establish 1111 International School Funds;
3. J)fridc to provide to the Fund, for a period of five years, continued financial assistance that the Assem› hlee generale may deem necessary;

4. ])fridc to vt'rsse in the Fon<ls in 1960 a contribution of 100,000 dollars to fill the operating deficit accumulated at the end of the school year 1959-1960 and for any other object concerning !' School which the Board of Directors will consider appropriate;

5. Requests the Secretary General to continue to give his good offices to the Board of Directors of !' School to help find perrnanent premises suitable for !' School has immediately proximate of the Siege of !' United Nations, to consolidate my plans of a building and has continued his efforts to obtain from private sources Is necessary for the construction of the building and, if necessary. a !' acquisition of the land;
6. Prfr the Secretary General to present to the Assem› blce generale, at its fifty-fifth session:
(a) A report of the Board of Directors of the School on the establishment and operations of the Fund;

(b) Recommendations, together with the recommendations of the Advisory Committee on Administrative and Budgetary Questions, on future contributions to the Fund and on ways and means of financing such contributions, including the suggestion contained in paragraplws 8 (t < ) of the report of the Secretary-General;
(c) A report on the results obtained with regard to the search for permanent premises for the school.
